name: build_datasets
title: build_datasets
description:
Builds the specified datasets. This tool returns a single build submission ID. 

CRITICAL: Make sure to git commit and git push local changes before using this tool.
CRITICAL: After submitting the build request, you should repeatedly use the get_build_status tool to wait until the dataset builds have succeeded. When you are waiting before checking the status again, provide the user with links to view progress along for CI checks or dataset builds.

filePaths.description:
A list of file paths containing the transform to build. 
Each item in the array should be a file path relative to the top level of the git repository containing the transform. 
For example, if the transform is in the /home/user/repo/transforms-python/src/datasets/transform.py file, 
and /home/user/repo is the top level for the git repository (i.e. the .git folder exists in /home/user/repo), 
then you should provide "transforms-python/src/datasets/transform.py" as the filePath.

datasetRids.description: The list of dataset RIDs to build.

datasetIdentifiers.description:
Identifiers for the datasets to build. 
Prefer using the datasetRids identifier when you know all the output dataset RIDs you want to build. 
Prefer using the filePath identifier when you do not know all the output dataset RIDs for the transform you want to build (i.e. the output uses a path instead of a RID).

branchName.description: The git branch name on which to base the build

commitHash.description: The git commit hash on which to base the build.

name: get_build_status
title: get_build_status
description:
This tool will retrieve checks/build status. Use it when you want to check a status of a dataset build or CI status.

CRITICAL: If the CI checks OR the dataset builds have not completed successfully, you should wait for some time by sleeping (CI checks usually take about 2minutes, Dataset builds vary from 1minute to multiple hours) and then check the status again. It is important you continuously monitor until everything has succeeded so that the user does not have to.
CRITICAL: When you are waiting before checking the status again, provide the user with links to view progress along with CI checks or dataset builds.

buildRid.description: The RID of the build submission to get the status for

tag.description: The tag to check CI status for

name.description: The name of the branch to check CI status for

commitHash.description: Optionally limit the results to a commit hash

name: get_job_status
title: get_job_status
description:
Retrieves detailed status information for a specific job by job RID.

A job is a single unit of work within a build, such as:
- The execution of a Python, SQL, or Java transform
- The automatic ingestion of data from an external system
- The syncing of data to the Palantir Foundry ontology
- The export of data to an external system

Returns detailed information about the job including:
  - jobId: Job RID
  - buildRid: Build RID that this job belongs to
  - jobSpecId: Job spec RID
  - jobStatus: Current status of the job
  - jobStatusReason: Detailed reason why the job is in its current status (e.g., dependency failed, canceled by user, etc.)
  - submittedTime: ISO timestamp when job started
  - finishedTime: ISO timestamp when job finished (if completed)
  - inputDatasets: Array of input datasets with branch, datasetRid, startTransactionRid, endTransactionRid
  - outputDatasets: Array of output datasets with datasetRid and outputTransactionRid
  - jobDetails: Full error messages, logs, and diagnostic information
  - jobDefinition: Source code information including filePath, repositoryId, and commitHash (null if not a code-based job)

jobRid.description: The job RID to get status for

name: get_foundry_dataset_schema
title: get_foundry_dataset_schema
description:
Returns the definition of a Palantir dataset for a given **datasetRid** or **datasetPath** and for an optional **branch**.
* A dataset definition includes the **datasetPath**, **datasetRid**, and **dataset schema**.
* If the dataset has not been built on the provided **branch**, then this will fallback to the default branch (typically "master" or "main"). or None if the dataset has not been built on any branch.
* Only one of **datasetRid** or **datasetPath** needs to be provided. If both are provided, they will be validated for consistency.
* A **datasetRid** is expected to be a string in the format ri.foundry.main.dataset.abcd1234
* A **datasetPath** is expected to be a fully qualified path in the format `/Path/to/dataset_name`
* A **branch** is optional, if it is not provided, the default branch **master** will be used.

datasetPath.description: Optional: The **datasetPath** of the dataset to fetch the schema for.

branch.description: The **branch** of the dataset to fetch the schema for.

name: get_dataset_stats
title: get_dataset_stats
description:
Returns size and file statistics for a Foundry dataset.
* Returns the total **sizeInBytes** (and human-readable size), **numFiles**, **hiddenFilesSizeInBytes**, **numHiddenFiles**, and **numTransactions**.
* Useful for capacity planning, Spark profile selection, and understanding dataset scale.
* Only one of **datasetRid** or **datasetPath** needs to be provided. If both are provided, they will be validated for consistency.
* A **datasetRid** is expected to be a string in the format ri.foundry.main.dataset.abcd1234
* A **datasetPath** is expected to be a fully qualified path in the format `/Path/to/dataset_name`
* A **branch** is optional; if not provided, the default branch **master** will be used.

datasetPath.description: Optional: The **datasetPath** of the dataset to fetch stats for.

branch.description: The **branch** of the dataset to fetch stats for.

name: list_dataset_files
title: list_dataset_files
description:
Lists the files in a Foundry dataset for a given **datasetRid** or **datasetPath** and **branch**.

This tool is particularly helpful for datasets without schema to explore what files are contained inside.

* Returns a list of files with their logical paths, physical paths, sizes, and modification times.
* Only one of **datasetRid** or **datasetPath** needs to be provided. If both are provided, they will be validated for consistency.
* A **datasetRid** is expected to be a string in the format ri.foundry.main.dataset.{UUID}
* A **datasetPath** is expected to be a fully qualified path in the format `/Path/to/dataset_name`
* A **branch** is optional; if not provided, the default branch **master** will be used.
* Use **logicalPath** to filter to a specific directory or file within the dataset.
* Supports pagination via **pageToken** for large datasets.
* Hidden files (starting with '.') are excluded by default but can be included by setting **excludeHiddenFiles** to false.

datasetPath.description: Optional: The **datasetPath** of the dataset to list files for.

branch.description: The **branch** of the dataset to list files for. Defaults to 'master'.

logicalPath.description: Optional: A specific directory or file path within the dataset. If omitted, lists all files in the dataset.

pageSize.description: The maximum number of files to return per page. Defaults to 100, max 1000.

pageToken.description: Optional: Token for pagination (from previous response's nextPageToken).

excludeHiddenFiles.description: Whether to exclude hidden files (files starting with '.'). Defaults to true.
